sentences.grammar_note: 'besonders' is an adverb placed directly before the adjective 'gut' to intensify it.
'besonders' intensifies the predicate adjective 'nett'; no declension needed for adverbs.
'Das Essen' is neuter nominative; 'besonders' modifies the adjective in predicate position.

sentences.german: Das Hotel in der Stadt ist besonders schön.
sentences.english: The hotel in the city is especially beautiful.
sentences.grammar_note: 'in der Stadt' uses Dativ (feminine: die Stadt → der Stadt); 'besonders' modifies 'schön'.

sentences.german: Die Museen in Wien sind besonders interessant.
sentences.english: The museums in Vienna are especially interesting.
sentences.grammar_note: Plural subject 'die Museen' takes plural verb 'sind'; 'besonders' works the same with any adjective.

sentences.german: Heute ist das Wetter besonders warm und angenehm.
sentences.english: Today the weather is especially warm and pleasant.
sentences.grammar_note: Time adverb 'heute' in first position triggers verb-second (V2) word order: heute → ist → das Wetter.

sentences.german: Das Museum war besonders interessant, und ich habe viele Fotos gemacht.
sentences.english: The museum was especially interesting, and I took many photos.
sentences.grammar_note: Coordinating conjunction 'und' joins two main clauses; second clause uses Perfekt: habe + gemacht.

sentences.german: Ich habe das besonders schöne Schloss in München besucht.
sentences.english: I visited the especially beautiful castle in Munich.
sentences.grammar_note: Perfekt with 'habe...besucht'; 'besonders schöne' is attributive — adjective takes weak ending (-e) after 'das'.

sentences.german: Du kannst in diesem Markt besonders viele Souvenirs kaufen.
sentences.english: You can buy especially many souvenirs in this market.
sentences.grammar_note: Modal verb 'kannst' (können) sends infinitive 'kaufen' to the end; 'besonders viele' modifies the noun.

sentences.german: Die Altstadt ist besonders schön, aber sie ist leider sehr voll.
sentences.english: The old town is especially beautiful, but it is unfortunately very crowded.
sentences.grammar_note: Coordinating conjunction 'aber' connects two main clauses; both keep standard V2 word order.
